headline: From Nine Years to Days: New Saliva Tests Transform Endometriosis Diagnosis
meta_description: Two non-invasive tests approved by the NHS cut endometriosis diagnosis time from nine years to days, offering relief to millions of women.
summary_short: The NHS has approved two rapid tests that diagnose endometriosis in days instead of years, sparing women from invasive surgery and years of unrelenting pain.
body:
For nearly a decade, Ami Robertson from Glasgow lived with chronic pelvic pain while doctors told her it was irritable bowel syndrome. When she finally took a non-invasive saliva test at age 23, the answer came in less than an hour. "For the first time, I was believed," she says.

Robertson's experience reflects a widespread problem: endometriosis, which affects one in ten women of reproductive age in the UK, took an average of nine years to diagnose—usually requiring laparoscopy, an invasive procedure under general anesthesia.

Now the NHS has approved two new tests that change everything. Endotest analyses saliva samples for microRNA markers, while the 45-minute EndoSure test measures electrical signals in the gut using sensor pads on the abdomen. Results from EndoSure come immediately after the test concludes.

For 15-year-old Simran Chavda, who experienced severe pelvic pain at age 13, the non-invasive test provided the evidence her mother—a GP—needed to secure a diagnosis of widespread endometriosis. "Getting my diagnosis honestly felt like the best thing in the world," Simran says.

Dr. Gail Busby, a gynecologist at Manchester University NHS Foundation Trust, calls the breakthrough "a game-changer." By delivering answers without surgery, these tests don't just transform individual lives—they free up surgical slots for everyone waiting for care.
